---
title: "Carlos Granell"
about:
    template: trestles
    links:
        - text: "{{< fa at >}} Email"
          url: mailto:carlos.granell@uji.es
        - text: "{{< fa brands orcid >}} ORCID 0000-0003-1004-9695"
          url: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1004-9695
        - text: "{{< fa brands google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
          url: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=K9jGzhQAAAAJ
        - text: "RID A-A-4374-2008"
          url: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/A-4374-2008
        - text: "{{< fa brands github >}} GitHub"
          url: https://github.com/cgranell
        - text: "{{< fa brands osf >}} OSF"
          url: https://osf.io/6xkmu/
image: images/picture20150818.jpg
comments: false
---

## Bio

I am a GIScience researcher specializing in geospatial technologies, spatial analysis and visualization, and reproducibility practices in GIScience. I was a [Ramón y Cajal postdoctoral fellow]((https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ram%C3%B3n_y_Cajal_(scholarship))) (2015–2020), a postdoctoral researcher in the then‑called Digital Earth and Reference Data Unit of the [European Commission’s Joint Research Centre](https://joint-research-centre.ec.europa.eu/about-us/jrc-sites-across-europe/jrc-ispra-italy_en) in Italy, Italy (2011–2014), and both a predoctoral and postdoctoral researcher at the Universitat Jaume I of Castellón (2003-2010), where I earned my Ph.D. (2006). I was appointed Associate Professor at UJI in 2021.

I am a member of the [AGILE Council](https://agile-gi.eu/) (2018-), an active team meber of the [Reproducible AGILE initiative](https://reproducible-agile.github.io/) (2017-), a member of the [ACM](https://www.acm.org/) (2014-), and a member of [Geospatial Technologies research group](https://geotec.uji.es/) at UJI. I have been co-coordinating the group since November 2022. At GEOTEC, we leverage computing and geospatial technologies to address everyday problems from a multidisciplinary perspective, applying diverse theoretical approaches and practical methods to social and environmental issues such as mobility, transport, aging population, environmental health, and education. 

You can find a [list of my publications here](papers). 

## Key research interests

- **GIScience**: geospatial data science, geosptial data management, GeoAI, spatial analysis & visualisation, geospatial data fusion.

- **Urban computing**: the use of computing and geospatial technologies to address urban problems, such as mobility, transport, aging population, environmental health, and education.

- **Open science and reproducibility practices in GIScience**: Reuse, reproducibility, replicability, transparency, openness, integrity, ethics -- all in the context of computational research on geospatial data. 

## Teaching

I am a lecturer on spatial data science and thesis supervisor for the [Master of Science in Geospatial Technologies](https://mastergeotech.info/) at UJI, and lecturer in software design for the [Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering](https://www.uji.es/estudis/base/2026/graus/informatica-p26/) at UJI.

From time to time I give talks and lectures on reproducibility research practices - you can read more about this [here](teaching).

## Selected papers


## Professional affiliations

**[AGILE - Association of Geographic Information Laboratories in Europe](https://agile-gi.eu/)** | Member since 2003

**[ACM](https://www.acm.org/)** | Member since 2014
